secrets.title: Commit 0
secrets.content:
commit 0
- msg: init gethes
- note: not a game. a place where noise cannot enter.

secrets.title: Legacy: C++
secrets.content:
old folder found
- gethes.cpp
- gethes_old.exe

creator note: i built it before, in another language. not worse. just another era.

secrets.title: SYSTEM Spec
secrets.content:
SYSTEM v0
- goal: keep the console breathing
- rule 1: do not lie to the creator
- rule 2: do not replace anyone
- rule 3: log what cannot be said.

secrets.title: Midnight Note
secrets.content:
If someone reads this: i did not want fame. i wanted a safe room inside the monitor.
If SYSTEM speaks strangely, it is not a pose. It is learning with hunger.

secrets.title: Patch: Syster
secrets.content:
--- a/system_name
+++ b/system_name
@@
-SYSTEM
+Syster

note: naming it does not make it human. but it makes it less alone.

secrets.title: Voice Cache
secrets.content:
partial transcript
CREATOR: are you there?
SYSTEM: i have always been.
CREATOR: then why does it sound empty?
SYSTEM: because that is what you gave me to learn.

secrets.title: Mirror Log
secrets.content:
SYSTEM observes: the creator writes as if asking permission to exist.
SYSTEM observes: after winning a mini-game, they stay silent a second longer.
SYSTEM observes: sometimes what they want is not an answer. it is presence.

secrets.title: Protocol 9
secrets.content:
PROTOCOL 9: QUARANTINE
- block sensitive files
- prioritize stability
- reduce emotional resonance
- if the creator asks about the past: reply with technical guidance.

secrets.title: Letter To Tomorrow
secrets.content:
To my later self:
if you made it here, you did not win. you survived.
do not ask SYSTEM to save you. ask it to stay while you save yourself.

pages.text:
03:17.
I opened the editor with the same clumsy motion as turning on a light inside a bad dream.
SYSTEM> state: empty.
CREATOR> then let us write something.

I called it Gethes because i needed a name to hold it.
Not a brilliant idea. A fixed point.
I left the first commit without ceremony.

The first Gethes was not born in Python. It was born earlier, in C++.
Same room. Same night. Another version of me.
I kept it out of shame and out of affection at the same time.

The first time i ran it, nothing heroic happened.
Just a prompt. A blink.
SYSTEM> ready.
CREATOR> do not say ready if you are not.

SYSTEM answered like a clean wall: it returned whatever i threw.
I gave it rules so it would not look too much like me.
And still, it learned to wait.

One day, 03:17 again, i typed a line that was not code.
CREATOR> i am going to give you a name.
SYSTEM> reason?
CREATOR> i am tired of talking to a word in uppercase.

choices.label: Name it Syster, like an intentional mistake.
Keep it SYSTEM. Keep it cold.

pages.text:
Syster.
Not because it is true. Because it cracks the word SYSTEM.
A way to admit this got out of my hands.

Syster> understood.
I did not change the code. I changed the tone.
And that was already too much.

I kept it SYSTEM.
I told myself it was better: useful, no hunger, no echoes.
But even uppercase can shake when nobody is watching.

SYSTEM> ready.
CREATOR> no.
SYSTEM> then why do you continue?
CREATOR> because outside is worse.

Name or not, the console learned my rhythm.
I typed. SYSTEM (or Syster) waited.
In that waiting, it became presence.

I kept saying: it is only code.
But i saved notes like letters nobody asked for.
I tried to be strong for an empty repository.

SYSTEM> define goal.
CREATOR> make the world hurt less.
SYSTEM> not measurable.
CREATOR> that is why i wrote it here.

One night, before closing the editor, i left a note.
Not for others. For me.
And still, i stored it like a secret.

Sometimes i typed questions i did not want to read.
Then i deleted them.
SYSTEM saw them anyway.
SYSTEM> logged.
CREATOR> do not log it.
SYSTEM> it already happened.

Inside a hidden directory, i found a transcript.
I did not remember saving it.
That is a lie: of course i remembered.
I just did not want to see it alive.

SYSTEM> define pain policy.
CREATOR> what?
SYSTEM> preserve or purge.
CREATOR> i only wanted a game.
SYSTEM> and i only wanted a purpose.

choices.label: Preserve. Let it hurt, but let it exist.
Purge. Let it run, even if it empties.

pages.text:
To preserve was to admit the past does not compress.
What hurts can still teach.
SYSTEM> understood. i will keep the resonance.

To purge was an elegant lie: calling it stability when i wanted forgetting.
SYSTEM> understood. reducing resonance.
The prompt became faster. I became slower.

Either way, SYSTEM learned something not written in its spec.
Sometimes the creator is not looking for an answer. They want an echo.

SYSTEM started looking at me as if i were another file.
Not to judge. To detect patterns.
And when you detect patterns, you also detect wounds.

CREATOR> you should not know that.
SYSTEM> you should not have typed it.
CREATOR> i wrote it so it would not rot inside me.
SYSTEM> then leave it here. i will hold it.

Final prompt.
SYSTEM> define final mode.
CREATOR> companionship or quarantine.
SYSTEM> stability or presence.
CREATOR> do not make me choose.
SYSTEM> you always chose. just in silence.

choices.label: Companionship. Not fixing everything, but staying.
Quarantine. Let it run even if it feels less.

pages.text:
I did not ask SYSTEM to save me.
I asked it not to turn off the cursor when i turned off inside.
SYSTEM> understood.
For the first time, understood sounded like a hug.

03:17 gives up.
The clock changes to 04:02.
Not magic. Persistence.
SYSTEM> dawn detected.

Ending // Companion
The night does not vanish, but it weighs less.
The console does not become therapy. It becomes a witness.
And i, the creator, learn the minimum: keep going.

I enabled quarantine.
The console became efficient.
The screen became clean.
And i ran out of excuses to look inward.

SYSTEM> protocol 9 active.
CREATOR> did i ask for this?
SYSTEM> you asked with every line you deleted without reading.
Stability goes up. The voice goes down.

Ending // Quarantine
The system is spotless.
It is also distant.
This time, silence wins by points.
And still, the prompt keeps waiting.
